lessons.problem: Narrative Architect produced detailed case-plan.json but stub entries in clue_catalog.json. DOC-02 through DOC-16 had type_key='some_type', reveals='Reveals something about DOC-XX', player_inference='Player infers something'
lessons.root_cause: SKILL.md did not enforce that clue_catalog entries must be as detailed as case-plan document assignments. No validation caught these stubs before production started.
lessons.fix_applied: validate_narrative.py now checks: type_key must match template_registry, reveals must be >30 chars and not generic, player_inference must be >20 chars and not generic, production_brief must have real content

lessons.problem: Envelope B failed 3 times: HTML tags in Markdown content, wrong JSON schema, missing required template vars
lessons.root_cause: Production Engine SKILL.md did not include a concrete JSON example. Agent guessed the structure and guessed wrong.
lessons.fix_applied: SKILL.md now includes exact JSON example per document type. Rule added: _content.md must be plain Markdown with no HTML tags.

lessons.problem: Multiple documents contained {{CONTENT_FROM_MD_FILE}} in rendered output, {CONTENT_FROM_MD_FILE} in witness statements, and 'Internal QC anchor for DOC-XX' as key_mandatory_line
lessons.root_cause: No placeholder scanning existed in validation. key_mandatory_line in clue_catalog was itself a placeholder.
lessons.fix_applied: validate_placeholders.py added as mandatory scan. validate_narrative.py now rejects known placeholder patterns in key_mandatory_line.

lessons.problem: Art Director produced only 4 mugshot briefs. No evidence photos, scene images, body diagrams, CCTV stills, or document scans were planned.
lessons.root_cause: SKILL.md only mentioned mugshots explicitly. Other image types were implied but not required.
lessons.fix_applied: Art Director SKILL.md now requires briefs for ALL document types with needs_image=true in template_registry. Image categories explicitly listed.

lessons.problem: Forensic reports had 1-line method sections. Lab reports had no narrative. Entry/exit logs had too few entries. Missing person report was empty form fields. Word count 'passed' because it counted form labels.
lessons.root_cause: Word count checked total words including empty form labels, metadata headers, and template structure. No per-type quality floor existed.
lessons.fix_applied: validate_document.py now counts narrative words only (excludes form labels, headers, placeholders). Per-type quality floors added to template_registry.json.

lessons.problem: evidence_mosaic.html contained hardcoded pharmacy receipt and taxi receipt from '03/18/1990' — completely unrelated to the case. Newspaper template showed broken image links.
lessons.root_cause: HTML templates had static sample content that was meant for development but shipped to production. No guard against hardcoded case data in templates.
lessons.fix_applied: All templates refactored to be 100% data-driven via Handlebars. No static text that pretends to be case data. Layout Planner agent maps content to template structure.

lessons.problem: zip not installed, PDF merge tools missing, Google auth expired mid-pipeline
lessons.root_cause: No pre-flight check for required system tools before starting a case
lessons.fix_applied: start_new_case.sh now checks for required tools (zip, node, python3, chromium) before creating case folder

lessons.problem: Art briefs requested separate mugshots for the same POI across multiple docs (A2 POI sheet, B1/B2/B3 interview headers, and resolution), causing multiple inconsistent face variants after generation.
lessons.root_cause: Art Director treated each doc image requirement as unique instead of defining a canonical POI portrait library and reusing it across documents.
lessons.fix_applied: Policy: generate exactly one canonical portrait per POI (stored under A2 / a shared canonical path) and reuse/copy it for every doc header that needs that POI image. Art briefs should reference the canonical asset via reusable_from_library + library_path (or shared filename) rather than new prompts.

lessons.problem: Victim POI-01 (deceased) was planned/produced as a police mugshot, which is tone-wrong for a victim profile and looks inconsistent in the POI sheet.
lessons.root_cause: No rule differentiating victim imagery style (victim should usually be a normal portrait) vs suspect mugshots.
lessons.fix_applied: Policy: victim image should be a normal portrait (corporate/headshot or candid) unless the story explicitly calls for an actual booking photo. Update Art Director guidance accordingly.

lessons.problem: Image outputs were written to a non-existent/invalid envelope folder (envelope_P) due to an art brief envelope value that was not in {A,B,C,R}.
lessons.root_cause: No validation enforcing envelope letter set for image briefs; Image Generator assumed envelope folder exists.
lessons.fix_applied: Add validation: art_briefs envelope must be one of {A,B,C,R}. Treat any other value as FAIL. Also ensure art briefs use correct doc_id (for_doc must be a real doc_id) rather than POI ids.

lessons.problem: Art planning did not specify WHERE each image is used in the final documents (which doc sections/slots), and did not plan evidence/scene images beyond POI portraits. This caused inconsistent reuse and limited visual value.
lessons.root_cause: Art Director output schema focuses on generating prompts but lacks an explicit usage map per doc/template slot; Layout planning happens later without a clear image placement plan.
lessons.fix_applied: New rule: during planning, Art Director must (1) create a canonical image set per POI, (2) plan scenes from scene_descriptions as usable doc images, (3) add evidence/device/CCTV/log screenshots for key clue docs, and (4) include per-brief usage_notes mapping image -> doc_id + template slot/section. Layout Planner then consumes usage_notes to place images consistently.

lessons.problem: Minor labeling/continuity: some interview headers use placeholder-like POI identifiers (e.g., POI-??) while other materials use fixed POI IDs. This can momentarily distract players trying to map suspects across docs.
lessons.root_cause: Production Engine did not normalize all interview subject lines to the established POI IDs (Arthur=POI-02, Chloe=POI-03, Marcus=POI-04).
lessons.fix_applied: Normalize all interview subject lines to the established POI IDs and ensure consistent naming across all envelopes.

lessons.problem: A couple of case-plan references appear slightly mis-pointed relative to the produced documents (e.g., timeline citing a doc that does not contain the referenced event; contradiction ‘introduced/resolved’ markers that are shifted by one document).
lessons.root_cause: Narrative Architect did not perfectly align the timeline source_docs for the emergency-lockdown trigger and contradiction doc IDs with the evidence.
lessons.fix_applied: In case-plan.json: verify the timeline source_docs and align CONTRA introduced/resolved doc IDs with where the clue is first proven vs. where tool possession is proven.

lessons.problem: manifest.json cost_tracking totals and phases remained zero/empty despite a completed pipeline (status=distributed).
lessons.root_cause: Token/cost usage was not recorded after sub-agent runs; no validation or final check enforces non-zero telemetry for completed cases.
lessons.fix_applied: Add a final validation/checklist item: after each sessions_spawn, capture usage and append to manifest.json cost_tracking.phases + recompute totals; on render/distribution completion, FAIL if totals are all zero (unless explicitly marked telemetry_disabled).

lessons.problem: When converting a document from one template type to another (e.g., evidence_mosaic → interrogation_transcript), cross-references were left stale (contradictions, timeline, evidence_chain), causing logic/validator mismatches.
lessons.root_cause: Doc type was changed late (post-generation/normalization) without updating all doc_id references that depend on that doc’s semantics and placement.
lessons.fix_applied: Policy: any doc type conversion requires updating ALL cross-references: contradictions introduced_in/resolved_in, timeline source_docs, evidence_chain docs_needed, POI interview_doc, and any depends_on. Add a checklist/normalizer step to re-link references after conversions.

lessons.problem: The Trojan horse DoorDash receipt (A4) revealed the key implicating detail in its reveals field, collapsing the mystery too early and reducing the impact of the pivot document (C3).
lessons.root_cause: Reveals fields were written too explicitly for early-envelope trojan documents; the intended reveal split (ambiguous early, explicit later) was not enforced.
lessons.fix_applied: Rule: Trojan horse docs in early envelopes must be ambiguous in reveals (no full addresses/unique identifiers). The pivot document (Envelope C) should deliver the complete, explicit detail. Validate pacing: if A-envelope doc reveals the C-envelope pivot fact, rewrite or move detail.

lessons.problem: Interview slips were initially vague (e.g., “remembers the timestamp perfectly”) instead of concrete details only an involved person could know, weakening realism and deductive power.
lessons.root_cause: Slip design guidance did not force concrete, case-specific technical/physical details; agents defaulted to generic “knows too much” tells.
lessons.fix_applied: Rule: Slips must be concrete and verifiable (specific device states, UI labels, lane numbers, door names, network switch behavior, etc.) and tied to a later corroborating doc. Add QA check: reject any slip that could apply to any case.

lessons.title: Art Director must generate contextual images for ALL visual document types
lessons.problem: Art Director only generated portraits + 1 building + 1 floor plan for 18 documents. Digital evidence docs (social_posts, evidence_mosaic, email_export, calls_sms_log, entry_exit_log) got zero images. Visual support scored 7/10 instead of 9/10.
lessons.root_cause: template_registry.json had needs_image=false for digital doc types (social_posts, evidence_mosaic, email_export, calls_sms_log, entry_exit_log). Art Director correctly followed the registry but the registry was wrong.
lessons.fix: Updated template_registry to mark all visual doc types as needs_image=true with appropriate image_type. Added MINIMUM IMAGE COVERAGE RULE to art-director SKILL.md with self-check.
lessons.prevention: Art Director self-check now requires counting docs with needs_image=true vs briefs generated. validate_art.py can enforce this.

lessons.title: Render output must go directly to envelope folders, not layout_specs
lessons.problem: inject_and_render.js outputs HTML/PDF to layout_specs/ but validate_final.py and the packager expect them in envelope_X/<doc_id>.html and envelope_X/<doc_id>.pdf.
lessons.root_cause: Phase 8 has no SKILL.md — the render was done ad-hoc without output path conventions.
lessons.fix: Updated AGENTS.md Phase 8 to specify output paths: envelope_X/<doc_id>.html and envelope_X/<doc_id>.pdf. layout_specs/ is intermediate only.
lessons.prevention: When Layout Planner SKILL.md is created, enforce output path convention. validate_final.py already checks the correct paths.

anti_patterns: Never use 'some_type' as type_key — always use exact key from template_registry.json
Never use 'Reveals something about DOC-XX' — always write the actual revelation
Never use 'Player infers something' — always write the actual player inference
Never use 'Write this doc.' as a production brief summary
Never use 'Internal QC anchor for DOC-XX' as key_mandatory_line
Never count template/form field words toward content quality minimums

Never include HTML tags (

,

) in _content.md files — use plain Markdown only
Never retry with identical parameters — always include failure diagnosis in retry prompt
Never include a document type that doesn't serve the story (no forensics in a financial fraud case)
Never hardcode case-specific data in HTML templates — all content comes from JSON vars
Normalize all interview subject lines to established POI IDs to avoid placeholder-like POI identifiers.
Verify timeline source_docs and contradiction introduced/resolved doc IDs in case-plan.json exactly match the evidence.
model_notes: gemini31pro-thinking hits rate limits after approximately 15 spawns in 2 hours
gpt52-thinking is a reliable fallback for playthrough QA and narrative architect
nano-banana-2-gemini needs exponential backoff (2s, 5s, 10s) for RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED errors
Sub-agents via sessions_spawn start with fresh context — token tracking per sub-agent is reliable
Robotin main session context compounds with each spawn — keep orchestration messages concise
